title: Impa (adenosine 5'-phosphorimidazolide) Synthesis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e252h","text":"Synthesis helps to facilitate the chemical production process. As a mature performance of synthesis technology, carbohydrate synthesis service and enzyme synthesis service are easily accessed. Here is the protocol of impa (adenosine 5'-phosphorimidazolide) synthesis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":112,"length":30},{"key":1,"offset":147,"length":24}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"F91DB2E778C240A0960F052318C62FD7","url":"http://www.bocsci.com/carbohydrate-service.html"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B446F60D75A44DAF8577A8216A1B0A46","url":"http://www.bocsci.com/biosynthesis-of-chemicals.html"}}}}
steps: Materials: %s %s aaa Reagent Supplier Product # CAS # Adenosine 5 -monophosphoric acid (5 -AMP) MP Biomedical 210008001 18422-05-4 Dimethylformamide (DMF) Sigma D-4551 68-12-2 Triphenylphosphine Sigma T84409 603-35-0 2,2 -Dipyridyldisulfide Sigma/Fluka 43791 2127-03-9 Imidazole Sigma I2399 288-32-4 Triethylamine Sigma T0886 121-44-8 Sodium perchlorate Sigma 410241 7601-89-0 Acetone Sigma 154598 67-64-1 Anhydrous ethyl ether Sigma 346136 60-29-7 Thin layer chromatography (TLC) cellulose plates with a 254-nm fluorescence indicator (cellulose-F TLC) Analtech 06011 (NH4)2SO4 Sigma A4418 7783-20-2 100% ethanol Gold Shield MgCl2 Hexahydrate Enzyme Grade Fisher BP214-500 7791-18-6 DTT glycerol Fisher BP229-1 56-81-5 HEPES VWR VWR1481-04 7365-45-9 acetylated bovine serum albumin Sigma B8894 9048-46-8 KOH 1. Dissolve 174 mg (0.5 mmol) of 5'-AMP in 15 ml DMF. Keep a 50-μl aliquot for TLC analysis.
Precipitate the ImpA by adding the reaction mixture dropwise into a vigorously stirred solution of 1.1 g (9 mmol) sodium perchlorate, 115 ml acetone, and 55 ml anhydrous ethyl ether.
A large glass pipet connected to a pipetting aid may also be used to aspirate off the supernatant.

Transfer the residual precipitate by rinsing the beaker with small volumes (5 ml) of acetone. Collect the precipitate by centrifuging 10 min at 3000 × g (5000 rpm with a Sorvall SS34 rotor), room temperature.
Soak cellulose-F TLC plates in 10% saturated aqueous (NH4)2SO4 and dry the TLC plates in open air for 1 hr or blow dry.

The AMP should have the lowest retention factor (Rf = 0.26), ImpA should run faster (Rf = 0.4), and the components of the triphenylphosphine solution all run near the solvent front. that's the protocol of this chemical synthesis .
creator: Annie Wood
